fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Kentucky Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Kentucky - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity kentucky, how to transfer dietitian license to kentucky, dietitian compact kentucky, kentucky dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Kentucky Board of Licensure and Certification for Dietitians and Nutritionists

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The credential to practice dietetics in Kentucky. Dual licensure as a Dietitian and Nutritionist is also an option for the same $50 application fee.

steps.title: Create Kentucky Online Gateway (KOG) Account
steps.description: Register for a KOG account to gain access to the Board's eServices Portal, which is required for all online applications.

steps.title: Submit Online Application and Fee
steps.description: Log in to the eServices Portal via your KOG account, complete the online application form for new licensure, and pay the non-refundable application fee.

steps.title: Send Official Transcripts and Supervised Practice Verification
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from all post-secondary institutions and Form B (Verification of Supervised Practice Program) to be sent directly to the Kentucky Board of Licensure and Certification for Dietitians and Nutritionists.

steps.title: Verify CDR Examination Scores
steps.description: Request the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) to send your official examination scores directly to the Kentucky Board of Licensure and Certification for Dietitians and Nutritionists.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the provided instructions on the Board's website to complete the required fingerprint-based criminal background check through the designated vendor.

documents.detail: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
Must show your conferred master's degree. Can be sent securely via Clearinghouse to the board.
Documentation showing completion of at least 1,000 hours.

provisional_text: Kentucky does not offer a temporary or provisional license. Applicants must have passed the CDR examination prior to applying for licensure.
reciprocity_text: Kentucky allows licensure by endorsement. Applicants actively licensed in another state must provide verification of licensure and proof that they meet the same educational, supervised practice, and examination requirements (active CDR registration typically satisfies this).

faqs.question: How much does a Kentucky dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The application fee for initial licensure as a dietitian (or dual licensure as a dietitian/nutritionist) is $50.

faqs.question: When does the Kentucky license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Kentucky dietitian licenses expire annually on October 31st. The renewal fee is $50. There is a 60-day grace period (until Dec 31st) with late fees applied.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements?
faqs.answer: Licensees must complete at least 15 hours of approved continuing education every year prior to the October 31st renewal deadline.

faqs.question: Does Kentucky have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Kentucky. To work in Kentucky, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Kentucky?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Kentucky while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Kentucky allows licensure by endorsement. Applicants actively licensed in another state must provide verification of licensure and proof that they meet the same educational, supervised practice, and examination requirements (active CDR registration typically satisfies this).

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Kentucky
hero.dek: To apply for a Dietitian or Nutritionist license in Kentucky, register for a Kentucky Online Gateway (KOG) account and submit your application through the Board's eServices Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: First, register for a Kentucky Online Gateway (KOG) account. Once registered, log in to KOG and access the Board's eServices Portal through the provided link.
